Title: NKS1/ELMO4 is an integral protein of a pectin synthesis protein complex and maintains Golgi morphology and cell adhesion in Arabidopsis
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: middle lamella; primary cell wall; seed coat mucilage
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: cell adhesion; middle lamella integrity; cell wall structure and tissue integrity
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required to maintain HG accumulation and pectin-rich middle lamella integrity; loss of NKS1 reduces GalA-rich pectin/HG, decreases seed mucilage, disrupts cell adhesion, and impairs Golgi structure/function
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: plant-specific DUF1068 family protein; ELMO family; single-pass transmembrane protein
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Golgi apparatus, preferentially medial-Golgi; N-terminus cytosolic and DUF1068 domain in Golgi lumen
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ubiquitously expressed; phenotypes mainly evident in young etiolated seedlings/hypocotyls
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: QUA1; QUA2; ELMO1; ELMO2; GAUT9; QUL2
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NKS1 loss-of-function mutants show reduced hypocotyl elongation, cell adhesion defects, lower GalA in cell walls, and reduced seed coat mucilage, consistent with defective HG/pectin synthesis. 2. Functional fluorescent fusions localize NKS1 to the Golgi, mainly medial cisternae, and loss of NKS1 disrupts Golgi morphology and secretion. 3. Genetic and protein interaction evidence shows NKS1 acts in the same pathway/complex as QUA1 and QUA2 to support pectin synthesis.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout analysis (nks1-2, nks1-3); molecular complementation with GFP fusions; qRT-PCR/RT-PCR; subcellular localization and topology assays; cell wall monosaccharide profiling by HPAEC-PAD; TEM/electron tomography; secretion assay; co-IP/LC-MS/MS; BiFC; genetic interaction with qua2-1 and esmd1-1
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to manipulate pectin-mediated tissue adhesion, seed mucilage, fruit softening/abscission-related wall properties, and cell wall integrity; should be used cautiously because loss impairs growth, Golgi function, and tissue integrity
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss causes reduced seedling growth, shorter hypocotyls, secretion defects, Golgi deformation, and epidermal/cortical cell separation
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Etiolated seedling growth; high sucrose enhances anthocyanin phenotype; reduced osmotic potential/hard medium partially rescues adhesion and elongation defects; Brefeldin A used for localization assays
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NKS1 is not presented as a catalyst but as an integral structural/organizing component of a pectin synthesis protein complex. Genetic interaction with qua2 and suppression by esmd1 support action in a common wall-integrity pathway. No significant cellulose content change was detected.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: middle lamella; primary cell wall; seed coat mucilage
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports cell adhesion and pectin-rich wall integrity
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galacturonic acid residues from UDP-galacturonic acid to extend the homogalacturonan backbone
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key HG synthesis enzyme implicated in backbone formation; rate-limiting not established in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GT8 family galacturonosyltransferase-like protein; also referred to as GAUT8
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Defective QUA1 function is associated with reduced HG and cell adhesion defects; in this paper QUA1 is identified as a core NKS1-interacting HG synthesis enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. QUA1 is a known HG biosynthetic enzyme whose mutant phenotype resembles nks1. 2. QUA1 co-immunoprecipitates with NKS1 and QUA2, supporting a stable pectin synthesis complex. 3. BiFC and co-IP confirm strong physical interaction between QUA1 and NKS1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QUA1-GFP immunoprecipitation coupled to LC-MS/MS; heterologous co-IP; BiFC; comparative mutant phenotypes referenced within the study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for engineering HG content and cell adhesion, potentially in combination with NKS1/QUA2/GAUT9 to optimize pectin synthesis complexes
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Studied mainly in etiolated Arabidopsis seedlings; interaction assays also in Nicotiana benthamiana and Arabidopsis protoplasts
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Tight coexpression with NKS1; used as a central anchor of the proposed HG synthesis complex

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: middle lamella; primary cell wall; seed coat mucilage
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports pectin accumulation and cell adhesion
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: homogalacturonan methylation coupled to HG synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: midstream modification step coupled with backbone synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates homogalacturonan during biosynthesis in the Golgi, contributing to proper HG production/processing
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: homogalacturonan and/or nascent HG; S-adenosylmethionine as methyl donor
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: important HG synthesis/modification enzyme; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: qua2 mutants phenocopy nks1 and qua2-1 nks1-3 double mutants resemble single mutants, supporting common pathway action in HG synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. QUA2 is an HG methyltransferase genetically linked to NKS1 through nonadditive double-mutant phenotypes. 2. QUA2 is recovered in NKS1 and QUA1 interactomes, supporting membership in the same pectin synthesis complex. 3. Co-IP detects a weaker but present interaction between NKS1 and QUA2.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double-mutant analysis (qua2-1 nks1-3); co-IP/LC-MS/MS interactomics; heterologous co-IP
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for modifying HG deposition and cell adhesion, potentially together with complex components rather than singly
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutant phenotypes include growth and adhesion defects as discussed for qua mutants
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Etiolated seedling assays; interaction assays in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper notes that qua2 mutants may not alter bulk pectin methylesterification status despite reduced overall pectin synthesis, supporting tight coupling between HG synthesis and methylation
